trend: Strong bullish but conditional for the next 1-3 trading days while NIFTY holds 24150-24020; upside continuation needs acceptance above 24500 because 24500 is the highest Call OI resistance.
entry: Direction: buy dips or buy sustained move above 24250; entry zone 24150-24220 on dips while above 24020; expected probability about 66%; risk-reward about 1:1.7 to target1 and 1:2.5 to target2.
stoploss: Below 24020 on decisive intraday breakdown or below 23900 on closing basis
bullish_reasons: NIFTY spot is up about 1.0% around 24200-24212 and is holding above the 24000 breakout area.
Bank Nifty is up about 1.1% near 57885-57890, confirming broader index participation.
India VIX is falling sharply intraday around 12.5-12.6, down roughly 5-6%, supporting risk appetite.
Option-chain PCR is 1.0451, inside the 0.9-1.2 bullish band.
Change-OI PCR is very strong at 2.9322, indicating fresh put-side support.
Price is around or above max pain 24200 on the latest spot reference; change-OI max pain is lower at 24150.
DII cash flow remains positive at +2058 Cr as of 2026-07-09, absorbing FII cash selling.
GIFT Nifty was +0.51% at 24225.50 in the 09:47 IST snapshot, marginally above the +0.5 bullish threshold.
US markets closed green overnight, with Dow +0.27% and Nasdaq strength; Asian markets are broadly green with Shanghai, Hang Seng and Nikkei positive.
Market breadth is strongly positive, with NSE advances around 2010 versus 429 declines in the 09:37 IST ET snapshot.
Major news flow is supportive: TCS Q1 results, IT strength, positive global cues, and continued DII absorption.
bearish_reasons: FII cash flow was negative at -533 Cr on 2026-07-09.
FII index futures remain heavily net short at about -266251 contracts, keeping institutional positioning conflicted.
Highest Call OI is 24500, so upside can slow near 24390-24500 unless call writers unwind.
Brent crude is still up materially for the week on renewed US-Iran conflict risk despite an early Friday dip; this remains macro-negative for India.
USDINR remains elevated near 95.46 even though it is softer day-on-day, so currency risk is not fully gone.
Exact live VWAP, full strike-level option IV table, call/put unwinding table, and public CPR bands were unavailable from accessible text sources; these factors were scored conservatively or proxied.
